report_name: White House Mandates US Gov Agencies to Adopt Post-Quantum Cryptography

vulnerabilities.name: Vulnerability of Classical Cryptography to Quantum Attacks
vulnerabilities.description: Current public-key cryptographic algorithms, such as RSA and ECC, are vulnerable to being broken by future cryptanalytically relevant quantum computers, which is the threat this Executive Order aims to mitigate.

identities.name: White House
identities.description: Issued the Executive Order mandating the transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography for U.S. federal agencies.

identities.name: United States Government
identities.description: The entity whose federal agencies are mandated to transition to Post-Quantum Cryptography by 2030.

identities.name: Office of Management and Budget
identities.description: Tasked with providing guidance and managing enforcement for the PQC migration via the FISMA reporting process.

identities.name: National Security Agency
identities.description: Tasked with developing and disseminating practical guidance, standards, and best practices for the PQC migration.

identities.name: Department of Homeland Security
identities.description: Tasked with developing and disseminating practical guidance, standards, and best practices for the PQC migration.

identities.name: Department of Commerce
identities.description: Tasked via NIST to develop and disseminate guidance and standards for the PQC migration.

identities.name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
identities.description: Responsible for finalizing and providing the technical standards for Post-Quantum Cryptography that agencies must adopt.

identities.name: U.S. federal government agencies
identities.description: The collective group of organizations required by the Executive Order to migrate designated high-value assets to PQC standards.

identities.name: Private sector government contractors
identities.description: Entities that contract with the federal government and will be indirectly required to adopt PQC to maintain compatibility and meet security requirements.

identities.name: Donald J. Trump
identities.description: Signed the Executive Order on June 22, 2026, mandating the PQC transition.

relationships.target_name: Vulnerability of Classical Cryptography to Quantum Attacks
